---
title: "Danilo Freire"
pagetitle: "Danilo Freire"
body-classes: home-page
---

:::: {.home-hero}
::: {.home-hero-left}
![](assets/img/profile.jpg){.profile-img fig-alt="Photo of Danilo Freire"}

::: {.social-row}
<a href="mailto:danilo.freire@emory.edu" title="Email"><i class="bi bi-envelope-fill"></i></a>
<a href="https://github.com/danilofreire" title="GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=lB1WKOYAAAAJ" title="Google Scholar"><i class="ai ai-google-scholar"></i></a>
<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4712-6810" title="ORCID"><i class="ai ai-orcid"></i></a>
:::
:::

::: {.home-bio}
# Danilo Freire

<p class="affil">Visiting Assistant Professor · Data & Decision Sciences · Emory University</p>

Welcome! I am a visiting assistant professor at Emory's [Department of Data and Decision Sciences](https://datascience.emory.edu/), where I teach courses on experimental methods, data science computing, and AI for the social sciences.

My research looks at how developing countries solve collective action problems and reduce political violence, mostly in Latin America. I use survey and field experiments, machine learning, and administrative data, and my papers have appeared in journals including the *American Journal of Political Science* and the *British Journal of Political Science*. I am also an [EGAP Fellow](https://egap.org) and an Honorary Senior Fellow at the [University of Lincoln](https://www.lincoln.ac.uk/socialsciences/).

You can reach me at [danilo.freire@emory.edu](mailto:danilo.freire@emory.edu) or find my code on [GitHub](https://github.com/danilofreire).
:::
::::

```{=html}
<div class="wayfind-grid">
  <a class="wayfind-card" href="research.html">
    <span class="icon"><i class="bi bi-journal-text"></i></span>
    <h4>Research</h4>
    <p>Articles, working papers, and replication files.</p>
  </a>
  <a class="wayfind-card" href="teaching.html">
    <span class="icon"><i class="bi bi-mortarboard"></i></span>
    <h4>Teaching</h4>
    <p>Courses at Emory and elsewhere, with syllabi.</p>
  </a>
  <a class="wayfind-card" href="cv.html">
    <span class="icon"><i class="bi bi-file-earmark-person"></i></span>
    <h4>CV</h4>
    <p>Full academic history, grants, and references.</p>
  </a>
</div>
```
